rationale.R1.2: The abstract asserts 'substantial gains across every evaluation setting' and the conclusion calls clarification 'universally applicable,' but the underlying data show ClarifyASR is worse than baseline on three of six conditions (test-other, CHiME-6, VA-Log) and tied on a fourth; checking coherence between the per-condition numbers and the aggregate conclusions is central.
rationale.R1.3: The headline claim—'substantial gains over a strong non-clarifying baseline'—is load-bearing and demonstrably misaligned with the reported WER table: ClarifyASR wins on 2/6 conditions, loses on 3/6, and ties on 1; this evidence-claim gap could invalidate the section's core argument.
rationale.R1.6: A bare 'review this section' delegation normally encompasses prose quality; the section contains several rhetorical overreaches and hedging inconsistencies (e.g., attributing losses to 'noise band' without statistical support) that require sentence-level attention.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritize checking whether the reported WER numbers actually support the paper's claims and conclusions, or improving the prose and writing quality of the section—or both equally?
